eel: noun. Any freshwater fish of the order Anguilliformes, which are elongated and resemble snakes.
eke: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ekit.
elk: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Elkei.
else: adjective. Other; in addition to previously mentioned items.
ewe: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Ewe.
ilk: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ilongot.
isle: noun. An island, usually very small.
keel: noun. A large beam along the underside of a ship’s hull from bow to stern.
kiwi: noun. A flightless bird of the order Apterygiformes native to New Zealand.
lee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lyélé.
leek: noun. A vegetable of variety Allium ampeloprasum, having edible leaves and an onion -like bulb but with a milder flavour than the onion.
lees: noun. The sediment that settles during fermentation of beverages, consisting of dead yeast and precipitated parts of the fruit.
lei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lemio.
lek: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Leipon.
lewis: noun. A cramp iron inserted into a cavity in order to lift heavy stones; used as a symbol of strength in Freemasonry.
lie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Likila.
like: verb. To enjoy, be pleased by; favor; be in favor of.
lis: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lisu.
lwei: noun. a monetary unit of Angola used from 1975-1990, one hundredth of a kwanza.
see: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Seneca.
seek: verb. To try to find; to look for; to search for.
sei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Seri.
sel: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Selkup.
sew: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sewa Bay.
sike: noun. A gutter or ditch; a small stream that frequently dries up in the summer; the marshy ground or ditch through which such a stream
silk: noun. A fine fiber excreted by the silkworm or other arthropod (such as a spider).
skew: verb. To form or shape in an oblique way; to cause to take an oblique position.
ski: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sika.
sleek: adjective. Having an even, smooth surface; smooth
slew: noun. The act, or process of slewing.
week: noun. Any period of seven consecutive days.
weel: noun. A trap for catching fish; a weely.
wile: noun. A trick or stratagem practiced for ensnaring or deception; a sly, insidious artifice
wise: adjective. Showing good judgement or the benefit of experience.
